name: Baldwin, Stephen

name: Shanks, Rachel

evidence: Filing lists her as Consultant at Sotol Ventures LLC (Round Rock 78681). LoopNet profiles Rachel Shanks of Sotol Ventures as a commercial real estate broker in Georgetown TX; other listings tie her to land acquisition & development. Sotol Ventures LLC was registered in TX 11/2021, matching her giving window.

name: Baird Law Firm PLCC
resolved_employer: Baird Law Firm, PLLC (Charlie Baird, criminal defense; former Travis County 299th District Court judge and TX Court of Criminal Appeals judge)
evidence: Austin criminal-defense law firm founded by Charles 'Charlie' Baird, who retired as Travis County district judge in 2010 after earlier serving on the Texas Court of Criminal Appeals. South Austin office (filing zip 78745; firm listed at 78748 nearby). Donated to George Morales.

name: Alice Yi
resolved_employer: AAPI community organizer/advocate; founder Austin Asian Community Civic Coalition, co-founder Asian Texans for Justice, APAPA Austin; AAPI liaison to Rep. Lloyd Doggett
evidence: Alice Ming Fei Yi is a well-known Austin AAPI civic activist of ~30 years: founded the Austin Asian Community Civic Coalition (2019), co-founded APAPA-Austin (2013) and Asian Texans for Justice, and serves as AAPI community liaison to Congressman Lloyd Doggett. Donated to Commissioner Margaret Gomez; FEC record all-Democratic (lean 1.0).

name: Krusee Campaign
resolved_employer: Political campaign committee (most likely of former TX State Rep. Mike Krusee, R-District 52, Williamson County; later a transit/rail advocate)
evidence: Donor is a campaign committee, not an individual, so no employer/industry applies. Name and north-Austin/Williamson zip (78729) point to the committee tied to Mike Krusee, longtime Republican TX House member (District 52, 1993-2009) and champion of Austin's MetroRail. Contributed to Commissioner Jeff Travillion in 2024. Identity of the committee not independently confirmed, hence medium.

name: Martinez, Michael

name: Deyoung, Claire

name: Kramer, Kenneth
resolved_employer: Likely Dr. Ken Kramer (retired) — first director of the Sierra Club Lone Star (Texas) Chapter, Texas water-policy specialist/lobbyist
evidence: Best match is Dr. Ken Kramer, who led the Sierra Club's Texas (Lone Star) Chapter 1989-2012 and was its water-resources chair/lobbyist for decades. Donation to environmentalist Commissioner Brigid Shea fits, and his current work on the Brazos River basin / Region H water planning aligns with rural Central Texas geography (filing lists Kenney, TX 77452, Austin County). Name is common and residence not independently confirmed, so medium.

name: Simmons, Thomas
